report_name: Stealthy 'PowMix' Botnet Targets Czech Workforce with Evasive C2 Communications

threat_actors.name: PowMix Actor
threat_actors.description: The unspecified threat actor behind the PowMix botnet campaign, targeting professionals in the Czech Republic since at least December 2025. The actor demonstrates sophistication in social engineering and technical implementation, with a focus on stealthy C2 communications to evade detection.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Attachment
attack_patterns.description: The attack begins with a phishing email containing a malicious ZIP archive with a .LNK file, using compliance-themed lures to target professionals.

attack_patterns.name: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: Victims are tricked into opening a malicious Windows Shortcut (.LNK) file contained within a ZIP attachment, which initiates the execution chain.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: A PowerShell loader is executed by the malicious LNK file to decrypt and run the final PowMix payload directly in memory, evading file-based detection.

attack_patterns.name: Scheduled Task/Job: Scheduled Task
attack_patterns.description: The PowMix malware establishes persistence on compromised systems by creating a scheduled task to ensure it runs again after a reboot.

attack_patterns.name: Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: PowMix uses HTTP/S for C2 communications, embedding encrypted data in URL paths to mimic legitimate REST API calls and evade network signature detection.

malware.name: PowMix
malware.description: A botnet delivered via phishing that uses PowerShell for in-memory execution. It is designed for stealth, employing randomized C2 beaconing intervals and embedding encrypted data into URL paths to mimic legitimate API traffic.

tools.name: PowerShell
tools.description: Used as a loader to decrypt and run the final PowMix malware payload directly in memory.

tools.name: schtasks
tools.description: Used by the PowMix malware to create a scheduled task for persistence on compromised systems.

campaigns.name: PowMix Campaign
campaigns.description: An ongoing campaign active since at least December 2025, targeting the workforce in the Czech Republic with the PowMix botnet via phishing emails.

identities.name: Czech Professionals in HR, Legal, and Finance
identities.description: The primary target group for the PowMix botnet campaign, lured with compliance-themed phishing emails.

identities.name: EDEKA
identities.description: A legitimate brand impersonated in phishing lures to add credibility to the campaign targeting professionals in the Czech Republic.

infrastructure.name: PowMix C2 Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Command and control servers used by the PowMix botnet. Communications are designed for stealth, using randomized beaconing intervals and embedding encrypted data in URL paths.

indicators.name: PowMix PowerShell Loader Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: PowMix PowerShell Loader Execution
id: 9c5b1a3e-f8d2-4e1c-b9a5-6f7d8e9c0b1a
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of a PowerShell script with a bypassed execution policy, a technique used by PowMix's LNK-based loader.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/newly-discovered-powmix-botnet-hits.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-17
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.execution
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - '-ExecutionPolicy Bypass'
      - '-File'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of a PowerShell script with a bypassed execution policy, a technique used by PowMix's LNK-based loader.

indicators.name: PowMix Persistence via Scheduled Task
indicators.pattern:
title: PowMix Persistence via Scheduled Task
id: 3d2e1c0b-a9b8-4f7d-8e6c-5b4a3d2e1c0b
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a new scheduled task using schtasks.exe, which is the persistence mechanism used by the PowMix botnet.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/newly-discovered-powmix-botnet-hits.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-17
tags:
  - attack.t1053.005
  - attack.persistence
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\schtasks.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: '/Create'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of a new scheduled task using schtasks.exe, which is the persistence mechanism used by the PowMix botnet.

indicators.name: PowMix C2 URL Pattern
indicators.pattern:
title: PowMix C2 URL Pattern
id: 7a6b5c4d-3e2f-4d1c-a9b8-c7d6e5f4a3b2
status: experimental
description: Detects outbound web requests with URL paths matching the pattern used by PowMix for C2 communications, which embeds data to mimic REST API calls.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/newly-discovered-powmix-botnet-hits.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-17
tags:
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.command_and_control
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|re: '/api/v\d+/data=.*'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects outbound web requests with URL paths matching the pattern used by PowMix for C2 communications, which embeds data to mimic REST API calls.

relationships.target_name: Czech Professionals in HR, Legal, and Finance
